instruction: Create a cron job for the current user that runs every day at 2:30 AM to delete all .tmp files in /tmp older than 7 days. The cron entry should use `find /tmp -name '*.tmp' -mtime +7 -delete` as the command. Also create ~/Desktop/cron_setup_report.txt containing exactly: schedule=30 2 * * * and target=/tmp on separate lines.
source: 外部参考来源：Ubuntu manpage - crontab(5) (https://manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/en/man5/crontab.5.html)

command: import pyautogui; import time; time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.click({SCREEN_WIDTH_HALF}, {SCREEN_HEIGHT_HALF}); time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.hotkey('ctrl', 'alt', 't'); time.sleep(0.5)

result.command: cron_exists='false'; crontab -l 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'find /tmp' && cron_exists='true'; schedule_ok='false'; crontab -l 2>/dev/null | grep -q '30 2 \* \* \*' && schedule_ok='true'; command_ok='false'; crontab -l 2>/dev/null | grep -q "find /tmp -name '\*.tmp' -mtime +7 -delete" && command_ok='true'; expected=$(printf 'schedule=30 2 * * *\ntarget=/tmp\n'); actual=$(cat ~/Desktop/cron_setup_report.txt 2>/dev/null); output_ok='false'; [ "$actual" = "$expected" ] && output_ok='true'; echo "cron_exists=$cron_exists"; echo "schedule_ok=$schedule_ok"; echo "command_ok=$command_ok"; echo "output_ok=$output_ok"
